返回本批次

OpenTelemetry 配置文件进入公开 Alpha 版

OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha

opentelemetry.io 作者 tanelpoder 分数 157 评论 21 2026/03/27 00:14
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 自从 OpenTelemetry 首次推出 Profiles 以来，与跟踪、指标和日志并驾齐驱的持续生产分析的统一行业标准的势头一直在增长。今天，Profiling SIG 自豪地宣布 Profiles 信号已正式进入公开 Alpha 阶段，我们已准备好接受更广泛的社区使用和反馈。 面向所有人的生产性能分析 在生产中持续捕获低开销性能配置文件是一项已经使用了数十年的技术。它有助于解决生产事件，通过提高软件速度来改善用户体验，并通过使相同的工作占用更少的资源来降低计算成本。从历史上看，该行业缺乏用于持续分析的通用框架和协议，即使 JFR 和 pprof 等格式很流行。通过 OpenTelemetry Profiles，我们引入了用于生产分析的行业范围标准，具有真正的供应商中立性，并由社区和生态系统支持提供支持。要实现这一目标，需要几个组件：为分析数据创建统一的数据表示，与 pprof 等现有格式兼容。引入一种新颖的基于 eBPF 的分析器参考实现。使 Profiles 成为 OpenTelemetry 生态系统的有机组成部分，例如将其与 OTel Collector 集成。以上所有内容都在 Alpha 版本中得到了显着改进，所以让我们深入了解我们一直在做的事情！标准化数据表示创建统一的分析格式这是一项重大挑战，因为它必须成为跨不同环境的行业标准。工作组必须协调众多要求：采样与跟踪、本机与解释运行时、线路/内存大小效率和数据可读性之间的紧张关系以及其他类似方面。最终的 Profiles Alpha 格式提供了一个平衡的功能集，可以有效地捕获分析数据：堆栈表示经过重复数据删除，以便每个唯一的调用堆栈仅存储一次，从而允许对不同的分析数据进行有效编码。其他常见实体的字典表也允许有效的数据规范化。虽然主要侧重于对聚合数据进行编码，但该格式还允许捕获带时间戳的事件数据以支持用例，例如记录单个（即使是采样的）off-CPU 事件。资源属性允许使用附加信息来增强数据模型。字符串字典支持可将分析数据高效（线径小 40%）链接到发出相关日志、指标或跟踪的同一资源。分析样本可以进一步与跟踪 Trace_id / span_id 属性相关联，从而实现数据的跨信号关联。语义约定为最常见的分析特定属性提供定义。最初受到 pprof 格式的启发并与 pprof 维护者合作开发，OTLP 配置文件已发展成为一个独立标准，可满足OpenTelemetry 生态系统。原始 pprof 格式的数据可以在 OTLP 配置文件之间进行往返转换，而不会丢失信息。为此，现在包含了一个新的本地转换器，以确保无缝的互操作性。为了确保数据质量和易于采用，我们还发布了一致性检查工具。这样可以验证导出的配置文件是否符合 OpenTelemetry 配置文件的技术规范和语义约定。通过 eBPF 分析代理获得无摩擦的见解通过将其 eBPF 分析代理弹性捐赠给 OpenTelemetry 及其与 OTel Collector 的集成，每个 OpenTelemetry 用户都可以在 Linux 上进行低开销的全系统连续分析，支持最广泛使用的语言运行时，无需任何额外的仪器。Alpha 提供了许多重大改进版本：eBPF 代理现在用作 OpenTelemetry Collector 接收器，利用现有的 OpenTelemetry 处理管道来处理指标和 K8s 元数据，并作为官方收集器发行版提供。Go 可执行文件的自动目标符号化对 Node.js V8 的ARM64 支持对 BEAM (Erlang/Elixir) 的初始支持对 .NET 9 和 10 的支持对 OTel 中的 Ruby 展开和符号化配置文件进行修复和改进生态系统OpenTelemetry 是一个包含许多精心策划的部分的整体生态系统。像配置文件这样的新信号必须无处不在地集成，这样所有信号才能相互受益，这一点至关重要。 Alpha 版本在 OTel 领域的许多方面对该领域带来了多项改进。配置文件横向集成的一些值得注意的示例包括：OTel Collector 现在支持接收特定格式的配置文件数据或使用基础设施信息增强配置文件。pprof 接收器允许从 pprof 格式的文件接收配置文件。k8sattributesprocessor 允许使用以下功能增强配置文件：Kubernetes 元数据。OTTL 支持允许构建自定义规则来转换或过滤配置文件。更新了 OTLP 资源模型以允许高效的信息共享，包括更新 Collector 以透明地支持配置文件信号的这种优化。入门要了解有关 OpenTelemetry 配置文件的更多信息，您可以访问作为 OpenTelemetry 文档一部分的配置文件概念页面。开始实际部署的最简单方法是将 OpenTelemetry eBPF 分析器与支持 OTLP 配置文件的后端结合使用。由于信号仍在开发中，生产就绪的后端尚未出现，但多个供应商正在努力支持 OpenTelemetry Profiles。为了加快开发和实验，Elastic 开源了一个名为 devfiler 的桌面应用程序，它重新实现了 eBPF 分析器的后端（收集、数据存储、符号化和 UI）部分。请注意，devfiler 不是真正的生产后端，不应该这样使用。有关更多说明，请参阅 eBPF 探查器存储库。由...提供给您这样的项目涉及很多人。感谢所有使这一切成为可能的人，包括：Alexey Alexandrov (Google)Ivo Anjo (Datadog)Frederic Branczyk (Polar Signals)Roger Coll (Elastic)Dmitry Filimonov (Grafana Labs)Felix Geisendörfer (Datadog)Nayef Ghattas (Datadog)Jonathan Halliday (Red Hat)Dale Hamel (Shopify)Joel Höner (Zymtrace)Christos Kalkanis (Elastic)Florian Lehner (Elastic)Damien Mathieu (Elastic)Greg Mefford (Adobe)Tigran Najaryan (Splunk)Tommy Reilly (Polar Signals)Tim Rühsen (Zymtrace)Josh Suereth (Google)Timo Teräs (Elastic)Brennan Vincent (Polar Signals)下一步我们鼓励团队构建分析开始使用 OpenTelemetry 配置文件的工具和产品。您可以通过以下方式参与：在您的工具中添加 OTel 配置文件作为导出或接收选项。这已经发生了（例如 async-profiler）！测试 eBPF 代理和 OTel Collector（v0.148.0 或更高版本）对配置文件的支持并报告问题。或者甚至发送 PR！查看信号文档并提出可以改进的建议。请注意，由于版本处于 Alpha 状态，信号不应用于关键生产工作负载。有关详细信息，请参阅 OpenTelemetry 中 Alpha 成熟度级别的定义。与此同时，为了下一个里程碑，有许多令人兴奋的工作已计划和正在进行中：由于信号的相关性对于可观察性的成功至关重要，因此正在进行基于 eBPF 的代理（例如 OBI 和 Profiling Agent）之间共享信息的工作。符号化是每个生产分析堆栈的关键组件，因此我们正在讨论标准化 API（存储格式）并发布参考实现进程内 SDK 代码和 eBPF 代理之间共享运行时信息对于跨信号关联非常重要，可以回答诸如“99% 延迟时跟踪的关闭 CPU 事件是什么？”之类的问题。进程上下文和线程上下文共享 OTEP 正在努力实现这一点。为此，我们需要您的反馈！要联系我们，请在 OTLP 或 Profiling SIG 存储库中提交 GitHub 问题。它将有助于使信号满足行业需求，并稳步发展到下一个高度：Beta 和 GA 版本！

Original Summary

  1. Since OpenTelemetry first introduced Profiles, momentum has only grown towards building a unified industry standard for continuous production profiling, standing alongside traces, metrics, and logs. Today, the Profiling SIG is proud to announce that the Profiles signal has officially entered public Alpha, and we are ready for broader community use and feedback.Production profiling for allContinuously capturing low-overhead performance profiles in production is a technique that has been used for decades. It helps troubleshoot production incidents, improves user experience by making software faster and reduces computation costs by making the same work take less resources. Historically, the industry lacked a common framework and protocol for continuous profiling, even with formats like JFR and pprof being popular.With OpenTelemetry Profiles, we’re introducing an industry-wide standard for production profiling, with true vendor neutrality and powered by community and ecosystem support. There are a few components to making this a reality:Creating a unified data representation for profiling data, compatible with existing formats like pprof.Introducing a novel reference eBPF-based profiler implementation.Making Profiles an organic part of the OpenTelemetry ecosystem, such as integrating it with the OTel Collector.All of the above have been substantially improved in the Alpha release, so let’s dive into what we’ve been working on!Standardizing the data representationCreating a unified profiling format is a significant challenge, as it must serve as the industry standard across diverse environments. The working group had to reconcile numerous requirements: sampling vs. tracing, native vs. interpreted runtimes, the tension between wire/memory-size efficiency and data readability, and other similar aspects.The resulting Profiles Alpha format offers a balanced feature set that can efficiently capture profiling data:The stack representation is deduplicated so that each unique callstack is stored only once, allowing efficient encoding of diverse profiling data.The dictionary tables for other common entities also allow efficient data normalization.While primarily focused on encoding aggregated data, the format also allows capturing timestamped event data to support use cases such as recording individual (even if sampled) off-CPU events.Resource attributes allow augmenting the data model with additional information. String dictionary support enables efficient (40% smaller wire size) linking of profiling data to the same resource that emitted associated logs, metrics or traces.Profile samples can be further associated with the Tracing trace_id / span_id attributes, enabling cross-signal correlation of the data.Semantic conventions provide definition for the most common profiling-specific attributes.Originally inspired by the pprof format and developed in collaboration with pprof maintainers, OTLP Profiles has evolved into an independent standard that addresses the broad requirements of the OpenTelemetry ecosystem. Data in the original pprof format can be round-trip converted to/from OTLP Profiles with no loss of information. For that purpose a new native translator is now included to ensure seamless interoperability.To ensure data quality and ease of adoption, we are also releasing a conformance checker tool. This allows validating that the exported profiles adhere to the technical specifications and semantic conventions of OpenTelemetry Profiles.Frictionless insights with the eBPF Profiling AgentWith the Elastic donation of its eBPF profiling agent to OpenTelemetry and its integration with the OTel Collector, low-overhead whole-system continuous profiling on Linux with support of the most widely-used language runtimes without any additional instrumentation is available to every OpenTelemetry user.A number of significant improvements are available with the Alpha release:The eBPF agent now works as an OpenTelemetry Collector receiver, leveraging existing OpenTelemetry processing pipelines for metrics and K8s metadata, and is shipped as an official collector distribution.Automatic on-target symbolization of Go executablesARM64 support for Node.js V8Initial support for BEAM (Erlang/Elixir)Support for .NET 9 and 10Fixes and improvements to Ruby unwinding and symbolizationProfiles in the OTel ecosystemOpenTelemetry is a holistic ecosystem with many orchestrated parts. It’s critical that a new signal like Profiles integrates ubiquitously, so that all signals can benefit from each other. The Alpha release brings multiple improvements in this area across many dimensions of the OTel universe.Some notable examples of the horizontal integration of Profiles include:OTel Collector now includes support for receiving Profiles data in specific formats or augmenting profiles with infrastructure information.A pprof receiver allows receiving profiles from pprof-formatted files.The k8sattributesprocessor allows augmenting profiles with Kubernetes metadata.OTTL support allows building custom rules to transform, or filter profiles.OTLP Resource model was updated to allow efficient information sharing, including updating Collector to transparently support this optimization for Profiles signal.Getting startedTo learn more about OpenTelemetry profiles, you can visit the profiles concepts page that is part of the OpenTelemetry documentation.The easiest way to get started with an actual deployment is to use the OpenTelemetry eBPF profiler in combination with a backend that supports OTLP Profiles. As the signal is still under development, production-ready backends have not yet emerged but multiple vendors are working on supporting OpenTelemetry Profiles.To speed up development and experimentation, Elastic has open-sourced a desktop application named devfiler that reimplements the backend (collection, data storage, symbolization and UI) portion of the eBPF profiler. Note that devfiler is not a real production backend and should not be used as such. For further instructions, please refer to the eBPF profiler repository.Brought to you by…Projects like this involve many people. Thanks to everyone who made this possible, including:Alexey Alexandrov (Google)Ivo Anjo (Datadog)Frederic Branczyk (Polar Signals)Roger Coll (Elastic)Dmitry Filimonov (Grafana Labs)Felix Geisendörfer (Datadog)Nayef Ghattas (Datadog)Jonathan Halliday (Red Hat)Dale Hamel (Shopify)Joel Höner (Zymtrace)Christos Kalkanis (Elastic)Florian Lehner (Elastic)Damien Mathieu (Elastic)Greg Mefford (Adobe)Tigran Najaryan (Splunk)Tommy Reilly (Polar Signals)Tim Rühsen (Zymtrace)Josh Suereth (Google)Timo Teräs (Elastic)Brennan Vincent (Polar Signals)What’s nextWe encourage teams building profiling tools and products to start using the OpenTelemetry Profiles. Here is how you can participate:Add OTel Profiles as an export or receive option in your tool. This is already happening (e.g. async-profiler)!Test the eBPF agent and OTel Collector (v0.148.0 or newer) support for Profiles and report issues. Or even send PRs!Review the signal documentation and suggest what can be improved.Note that with the Alpha status of the release, the signal should not be used for critical production workloads. See the definition of the Alpha maturity level in OpenTelemetry for details.In the meantime and towards the next milestone, there is a lot of exciting work planned and in the works:As correlation of signals is crucial for the success of observability, there is ongoing work on sharing information between eBPF based agents, like OBI and Profiling Agent.Symbolization is a key component of every production profiling stack, so we are discussing standardizing the API, the storage format and publishing a reference implementation for it.Sharing of runtime information between in-process SDK code and eBPF agents is important for cross-signal correlation to allow answering questions like “What were the off-CPU events for traces at the 99% latency?”. Process context and thread context sharing OTEPs are in the works to enable this.And for all of this, we need your feedback! To reach us, file a GitHub issue in the OTLP or Profiling SIG repository. It will help make the signal fit the industry needs and steadily evolve it towards its next heights: Beta and GA releases!

评论（默认中文）

petterroea 2026/03/27 09:49
这太酷了，OTel 正在取得进展。

我发现 OTel 仍然存在一些缺陷，而且它还不是真正的一站式遥测服务，我们希望它能够发挥作用。特别是，还没有好的（哨兵式）异常捕获。他们最近还更改了很多指标名称（似乎有充分的理由），这破坏了您在互联网上找到的大多数仪表板。

有人警告我 OTel 还不成熟，我发现这仍然是事实，但现在维护者似乎正在尝试对此做点什么

This is cool, OTel is getting somewhere.

I've found OTel to still have rough edges and it's not really the one stop shop for telemetry we want it to be at $work yet. In particular, no good (sentry-style) exception capturing yet. They also recently changed a lot of metric names(for good reason it seems), which breaks most dashboards you find on the internet.

I have been warned by people that OTel isn't mature yet and I find it to still be true, but it seems the maintainers are trying to do something about this nowadays

在 HN 中查看
tuo-lei 2026/03/27 10:26
那么你有什么替代方案的建议（除了Sentry）吗？我确实觉得 OTel 在痕迹方面总体上有相当广泛的支持。
do you have any suggestions for alternatives then (besides Sentry)? I do feel OTel have pretty wide support in general in term of traces.
在 HN 中查看
petterroea 2026/03/27 12:36
我知道很多商店更喜欢 datadog 堆栈，它显然有自己的类似哨兵的异常捕获系统。对我来说，异常捕获是一个明显的核心功能，与同意、使用 datadog 并感到满意的人讨论 OTel 是一种耻辱。
I know a lot of shops that prefer the datadog stack, which apparently does have its own sentry-like exception capturing system. To me, exception capturing is an obvious core feature and it is humiliating to discuss OTel with people who agree, and use datadog and are satisfied.
在 HN 中查看
genthree 2026/03/27 01:11
相关：是否有人分析过 rsyslogd（Linux 和 FreeBSD 分布式系统记录器，也许还有其他平台）在将日志传送到中央节点的模式下的性能和可靠性特征？我已经配置并使用了相对较小的（高个位数节点，每分钟一百万个或两个请求左右的活动突发）设置，但想知道是否有原因它不是分布式日志记录和跟踪的更常见解决方案（是的，它不能解决这些问题的 UI 问题，但它确实解决了收集日志的问题）

就像……有人对 rsyslogd 做过类似 Jepsen 的压力测试并分享了结果吗？之前我也曾半途而废地寻找过，却没有发现任何东西。

Relatedly: Has anyone profiled the performance and reliability characteristics of rsyslogd (Linux and FreeBSD distributed syslogger, maybe other platforms too) in its mode where it’s shipping logs to a central node? I’ve configured and used it with relatively small (high single digit nodes, bursts of activity to a million or two requests per minute or so) set-ups but have wondered if there’s a reason it’s not a more common solution for distributed logging and tracing (yes it doesn’t solve the UI problem for those, but it does solve collecting your logs)

Like… has anyone done a Jepsen-like stress test on rsyslogd and shared the results? I’ve half-assedly looked before and not been able to find anything.

在 HN 中查看
jbaiter 2026/03/27 03:38
我们每天使用几十 GiB 的日志（rsylog ->central rsylog ->elasticsearch）来完成此操作。它工作可靠，但配置绝对是一场噩梦，文档是鱼龙混杂，故障排除通常需要深入研究 C 代码。我们计划迁移到 Alloy+Loki。
We're doing this with a few dozen GiBs of logs a day (rsylog -> central rsylog -> elasticsearch). It works reliably, but the config is an absolute nightmare, documentation is a mixed bag and troubleshooting often involves deep dives into the C code. We're planning to migrate to Alloy+Loki.
在 HN 中查看
ehostunreach 2026/03/27 07:17
由于这是与 OTel 相关的提交，因此您还可以使用 OTel 收集器来收集日志并将日志转发到中央 OTel 收集器实例。

> 是的，它并没有解决这些问题的 UI 问题，但它确实解决了收集日志的问题

我在 Netdata 工作，在过去的几个月里，我们开发了一个外部 Netdata 插件，可以提取/索引 OTel 日志 [1]。当前的实现将日志存储在与 systemd 兼容的日志文件中，我们的可视化效果实际上与查询 systemd 日志日志时得到的结果相同 [2]。 我 > 比如……有人对 rsyslogd 做过类似 Jepsen 的压力测试并分享了结果吗？之前我也曾半途而废地寻找过，却没有发现任何东西。

我没有专门使用过 rsyslogd，但我不明白您所描述的日志量有什么问题。

[1] https://github.com/netdata/netdata/tree/master/src/crates/ne...

[2] https://learn.netdata.cloud/docs/logs/systemd-journal-logs/s...

Since this is an OTel-related submission, you could also use OTel collectors to collect and forward logs to a central OTel collector instance.

> yes it doesn’t solve the UI problem for those, but it does solve collecting your logs

I work for Netdata and over the last couple months, we've developed an external Netdata plugin that can ingest/index OTel logs [1]. The current implementation stores logs in systemd-compatible journal files and our visualization is effectively the same one someone would get when querying systemd journal logs [2]. i > Like… has anyone done a Jepsen-like stress test on rsyslogd and shared the results? I’ve half-assedly looked before and not been able to find anything.

I've not used rsyslogd specifically, but I don't see how you'd have any issues with the log volume you described.

[1] https://github.com/netdata/netdata/tree/master/src/crates/ne...

[2] https://learn.netdata.cloud/docs/logs/systemd-journal-logs/s...

People don’t care about syslog. 98% of my colleagues haven’t heard of it.

SEJeff 2026/03/27 03:25
我想知道这与 Grafana Pyrscope 相比如何，Grafana Pyrscope 非常适合此类事情并且已经相当成熟：

https://grafana.com/oss/pyroscope/

https://github.com/grafana/pyroscope

I wonder how this compares to grafana pyroscope, which is really good for this sort of thing and already quite mature:

https://grafana.com/oss/pyroscope/

https://github.com/grafana/pyroscope

在 HN 中查看
ollien 2026/03/27 03:59
据我所知，Pyrscope 本身不是一个分析器，而是一个可以发送/查询配置文件的地方。 OpenTelemtry 正在发布一个分析器，因此它们不会进行比较。一个可以与另一个一起使用。
As far as I'm aware, Pyroscope itself is not a profiler, but a place you can send/query profiles. OpenTelemtry is releasing a profiler, so they don't compare. One can be used with the other.
在 HN 中查看
SEJeff 2026/03/27 08:52
它肯定有分析客户端库 https://grafana.com/docs/pyroscope/latest/configure-client/l...
It definitely has profiling client libraries https://grafana.com/docs/pyroscope/latest/configure-client/l...
在 HN 中查看
sciurus 2026/03/27 04:50
您可以将 opentelemetry 收集的配置文件发送到 Pyrscope。

https://grafana.com/docs/pyroscope/latest/configure-client/o...

You can send profiles collected by opentelemetry to pyroscope.

https://grafana.com/docs/pyroscope/latest/configure-client/o...

在 HN 中查看
AbanoubRodolf 2026/03/27 11:08
并不是真正的非此即彼。 Pyrscope 已经具有 OTel 兼容接收器，因此您可以直接将 OTel 配置文件发送到其中，无需任何格式转换。它们是互补的。

OTel 分析标准作为客户合同比后端选择更有价值。使用 OTel SDK 进行一次检测，然后路由到 Pyrscope、Grafana Cloud、Datadog 或您自己的 Tempo 实例，而无需更改应用程序代码。这就是实际的音高。

当前的摩擦并不是标准本身。这是语言覆盖范围的差距。通过 eBPF 的 JVM 连续分析非常可靠且可用于生产。 Node.js 仍然使用 V8 采样分析器，与内核级 eBPF 方法的不到 1% 的开销相比，这增加了 2-5% 的开销。这就是阿尔法中值得关注的差距。

Not really either/or. Pyroscope already has an OTel-compatible receiver, so you can send OTel profiles directly into it without any format conversion. They're complementary.

The OTel profiling standard is more valuable as a client contract than a backend choice. Instrument once with the OTel SDK, then route to Pyroscope, Grafana Cloud, Datadog, or your own Tempo instance, without changing application code. That's the actual pitch.

The current friction isn't the standard itself. It's language coverage gaps. JVM continuous profiling via eBPF is solid and production-ready. Node.js still falls back to the V8 sampling profiler, which adds 2-5% overhead compared to sub-1% for kernel-level eBPF approaches. That's the gap worth watching in the alpha.

在 HN 中查看
ollien 2026/03/27 02:47
对此感到非常兴奋。我们已经在 $WORK 中使用过该版本的 Elixir 几次，并且发现它非常有用。
Very excited for this. We've used the Elixir version of this at $WORK a handful of times and have found it exceptionally useful.

secondcoming 2026/03/27 00:48
> 持续捕捉生产中的低开销性能概况

令我惊讶的是，OTel 社区设计的任何东西都能满足“低开销”的期望。

> Continuously capturing low-overhead performance profiles in production

It suprises me that anything designed by the OTel community could ever meet 'low-overhead' expectations.

在 HN 中查看
tanelpoder 2026/03/27 01:19
分析器 [1] 的参考实现最初是由 Optimyze 团队构建的，后来 Elastic 收购了该团队（并捐赠给 OTEL）。该团队非常擅长他们所做的事情。例如，他们发明了 .eh_frame 遍历技术，可以在不启用帧指针的情况下从二进制文件中获取堆栈跟踪。

该团队的一些 OG 后来创立了 Zymtrace [2]，他们也在做同样的事情来分析发生的情况 里面 现在有 GPU 了！

[1] https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-ebpf-profile...

[2] https://zymtrace.com/article/zero-friction-gpu-profiler/

The reference implementation of the profiler [1] was originally built by the Optimyze team that Elastic then acquired (and donated to OTEL). That team is very good at what they do. For example, they invented the .eh_frame walking technique to get stack traces from binaries without frame pointers enabled.

Some of the OGs from that team later founded Zymtrace [2] and they're doing the same for profiling what happens inside GPUs now!

[1] https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-ebpf-profile...

[2] https://zymtrace.com/article/zero-friction-gpu-profiler/

在 HN 中查看
rnrn 2026/03/27 05:06
> 例如，他们发明了 .eh_frame 遍历技术，可以在不启用帧指针的情况下从二进制文件中获取堆栈跟踪。

这并不是他们开发内容的准确总结。

使用 .eh_frame 在没有帧指针的情况下展开堆栈并不新颖 - 这正是它的用途，并且自 2010 年以来 perf 已经有了一个实现。问题是内核对此的支持被反复拒绝，因此内核对堆栈的千字节进行采样，然后用户空间进行展开

他们开发的是使用 eh_frame 中的数据从内核中运行的 eBPF 程序展开的实现。

> For example, they invented the .eh_frame walking technique to get stack traces from binaries without frame pointers enabled.

This is not an accurate summary of what they developed.

Using .eh_frame to unwind stacks without frame pointers is not novel - it is exactly what it is for and perf has had an implementation doing it since ~2010. The problem is the kernel support for this was repeatedly rejected so the kernel samples kilobytes of stack and then userspace does the unwind

What they developed is an implementation of unwinding from an eBPF program running in the kernel using data from eh_frame.

在 HN 中查看
tanelpoder 2026/03/27 06:12
确实，我应该更具体地说明上下文：

他们的发明是将 .eh_frame 下推到内核空间，因此您不需要将大块堆栈内存发送到用户空间进行后处理。而eBPF代码就是那个“下推”的.eh_frame行走的执行者。

GitHub 页面也提到了一项相关专利： https://patents.google.com/patent/US11604718B1/en

True, I should have been more specific about the context:

Their invention is about pushing down the .eh_frame walking to kernel space, so you don't need to ship large chunks of stack memory to userspace for post-processing. And eBPF code is the executor of that "pushed down" .eh_frame walking.

The GitHub page mentions a patent on this too: https://patents.google.com/patent/US11604718B1/en

felixge 2026/03/27 01:13
OTel Profiling SIG 维护者：我理解您的担忧，但我们已尽力使整个协议和所有内容变得高效 涉及的组件。

如果您发现我们现在运送的商品有任何问题，请告诉我们。

OTel Profiling SIG maintainer here: I understand your concern, but we’ve tried our best to make things efficient across the protocol and all involved components.

Please let us know if you find any issues with what we are shipping right now.
